frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: predictive state representation cached over future-state occupancy under a policy

frameworks.name: reduced successor representation
frameworks.one_line: random-policy SR variant proposed to produce addiction-like habitual behavior

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: cached state-action values contrasted with (red)SR control in alcohol contexts

core_question: Do high-risk drinkers rely more on (reduced) successor representation strategies than low-risk drinkers, especially in alcohol-related contexts?
key_assumptions: SR and redSR are dissociable from model-based and model-free control in human behavior
context (alcohol-related vs neutral) modulates which RL strategy is recruited
redSR under a random policy can capture addiction-like habit signatures
key_findings: a reduced random-policy SR-like strategy contributes to human behavior
high-risk and low-risk drinkers do not differ in their use of (red)SR
both groups rely less on (red) random-policy SR and more on model-free control in alcohol-related contexts
interpretations: reduced random-policy SR supports adaptive, resource-efficient behavior
this SR-like strategy is selectively downregulated in substance-related contexts rather than being a stable addiction marker
